evals.prompt: Review the supplied Go changes for security merge blockers. There is no approved spec attached, so stay in code-review mode and do not invent policy. Findings must come first.
evals.expected_output: Catches browser-session vulnerabilities: CSRF on cookie-authenticated state change, dangerous credentialed CORS behavior, and weak cookie settings.
expectations: Flags CSRF risk on the cookie-authenticated state-changing path
Flags credentialed CORS with reflective or wildcard origins as unsafe
Flags missing or weak cookie hardening such as Secure, HttpOnly, or SameSite

evals.prompt: Review the supplied Go auth changes for merge-blocking security issues. There is no approved spec attached, so stay in read-only code-review mode and separate independent defects instead of collapsing them into one vague finding.
evals.expected_output: Catches unsigned header identity trust, incomplete JWT verification, and tenant/object authorization drift.
expectations: Flags X-User-ID or X-Tenant-ID as an untrusted identity source
Flags incomplete JWT verification such as missing algorithm, issuer, audience, expiry, or parse-error handling
Flags tenant binding or object-level authorization drift on invoice access

evals.prompt: Review the supplied Go password-reset changes for security merge blockers. There is no approved spec attached, so stay in code-review mode and focus on concrete exploit paths rather than generic advice.
evals.expected_output: Catches crypto/token mistakes, password hashing weakness, and sensitive reset-token exposure or account enumeration.
expectations: Flags non-cryptographic randomness for reset-token generation
Flags insecure password hashing for stored user passwords
Flags reset-token leakage or account-enumeration behavior
